mechanism_narrative: Sustained low level of the federal funds rate anchors short-term discount rates, reducing the opportunity cost of equity capital and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency < 55% across low-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9 percent indicates severe labor market slack that crushes corporate earnings expectations and household income growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC is greater than 0.0 over any 3-year rolling window containing such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous near-zero federal funds rates and elevated unemployment create a policy-backstop regime where the central bank's implicit put suppresses downside equity risk, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive during triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power versus main effects alone (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals a supply-side recovery in factory output and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as growth momentum is priced in.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 during post-shock recovery periods, or positive 5-day return frequency is less than 55 percent in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units reveal a frozen residential investment channel that depresses construction employment and durable goods demand, dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when HOUST is below 600, or Rank IC exceeds 0.0 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in payroll employment and industrial production confirm a synchronised recovery across labor and supply domains, and their interaction produces stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and INDPRO increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term does not significantly improve prediction accuracy versus additive main effects (F-test p > 0.05), or forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates below 25 basis points reflect a zero-lower-bound liquidity trap where safe yields vanish and investors are pushed into equities, compressing risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25, or Rank IC is negative in that regime over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contractionary labor market dynamics that trigger downward revisions to corporate revenue and profit forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC is greater than 0.0 over any 3-year rolling window.
